title: Rhaphiolepis salwinensis
taxonomy: Rhaphiolepis salwinensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. This plant is native to the Himalayas and is also known as the salwin rhaphiolepis. It belongs to the genus Rhaphiolepis, which is a group of flowering plants that are commonly found in Asia and eastern parts of the world. The genus Rhaphiolepis is further classified under the order Rosales and the class Magnoliopsida. Rhaphiolepis salwinensis is a small, deciduous shrub that produces white or pink flowers.

intro: Rhaphiolepis salwinensis is a plant species in the family Rosaceae, commonly known as the Salween hawthorn or Salween medlar.
